blocks.content: The game was something. I'll say that.

Jonathan was different on the field this time — not better exactly, but more settled. Like he'd stopped trying to prove something and was just playing. It looked easier. Those are usually the best performances.
Third quarter. First score. Larry Jr. was standing and I didn't even try to tell him to sit.

Jonathan found me in the stands after the second catch. Just for a second — the way athletes do when they need to connect to something outside the field to remind themselves why. He held it for a beat. I nodded.
That was the whole conversation. I needed it as much as he did.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Why does he look at you like that?

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Like you're a person he needs.

I didn't say anything. We watched the game. The lights stayed up and the crowd made its sounds and for a little while everything that was heavy went somewhere in the back where I could still feel it but didn't have to look directly at it.
